mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5% YoY while CPIAUCSL rises > 3% YoY, or interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in higher near-term corporate output and margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during monetary tightening phases, or the signal's predictive power reverses direction when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained GDP growth above 2.5% reflects strong aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150,000 confirm robust labor market expansion, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate higher wage growth and consumption.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 60% of months with PAYEMS growth above 150k, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with an increasing federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy response that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stable retail sales indicates efficient supply chain transmission to consumer markets, driving positive S&P 500 returns as corporate inventory turnover improves.
failure_condition: SPX returns turn negative when INDPRO rises while RSAFS declines, or the divergence signal loses predictive power during periods of high volatility (VIX > 25).
